findings.criterion: Scenario expresses the normal Markdown-rendering behaviour (valid input -> rendered HTML).

evidence.quote: When the user enters "# Heading" into the textarea with data-testid "note-textarea"

then: the main display area with data-testid "note-main" should render "

Heading

"

findings.reasoning:
The scenario includes a clear precondition (Notes page loaded and a new note added), the core action (entering "# Heading" into data-testid "note-textarea" and clicking the Edit button), and a concrete Then outcome asserting the main area renders "

Heading

". This directly matches the requirement that Markdown input should be rendered as HTML in the main display.

findings.criterion: Missing coverage for the edge contract: invalid/unmatched Markdown should display raw input on exit without crashing.

evidence.quote: invalid Markdown (e.g., unmatched **) does not cause crashes or errors, and instead displays the raw input when the user exits the edit mode by clicking the "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button").
When the user enters "# Heading" into the textarea with data-testid "note-textarea"

findings.reasoning: The provided behaviour contract requires an explicit edge-case: when the textarea contains invalid/unmatched Markdown (e.g., unmatched **), exiting edit mode must show the raw input and not crash. The current scenario only exercises a valid Markdown input ("# Heading") and its rendered HTML outcome; it does not exercise or claim the invalid-markdown-on-exit behaviour, so the contract is not covered by this scenario.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a separate scenario for the invalid-markdown edge case. Example: Given a note in edit mode; When the user enters "unmatched **bold" into data-testid "note-textarea" and clicks the "Edit" button (data-testid "edit-note-button"); Then the main display area (data-testid "note-main") should display the raw text "unmatched **bold" and the app remains responsive (no crash).
